dominant_mechanism: Parentification of visit-compliance decisions — Melissa explicitly frames court-ordered visit refusals as Olivia's autonomous choice, burdening the child with adult legal decisions

anchor_quotes: ROW-14097: 'She's told the counselor before how she's afraid to talk to you about things and how she feels cause you would just yell at her and not listen'
signals.note: First text-record evidence of therapeutic context weaponized in adult conflict. Counselor relay + child positioned as parental support referee.

anchor_quotes: ROW-14490: 'Why are you telling her she has to stay at your house when she wants to come home because the judge said so'
ROW-14508: 'She doesn't feel safe and supported as evidenced by the texts I got'
signals.note: Court order inverted as child-endangerment framing; Olivia's texts to Melissa during Eli's parenting time deployed as evidence.

anchor_quotes: CP2 Q4 confirmed: Melissa told Olivia about Eli's Nov 22 2025 police report filing. Olivia stopped talking to Eli for over a week.
signals.note: Behavioral impact documented. Child told father filed police report about her visit refusal — child made aware of legal enforcement action arising from her own behavior.

anchor_quotes: ROW-14841 area: 'It was her choice not to go and I am not expected to forcibly remove her from the home'
signals.note: Strongest text-documented parentification statement in the dataset. Court-order compliance explicitly placed on Olivia's decision-making. Melissa abdicates enforcement responsibility.

anchor_quotes: ROW-15102 (received): 'Her, Milo and Alice were hiding in her room. Milo was telling Olivia if it kept going they might need to leave and they discussed going out the window because they were both scared.'
ROW-15103 (received): 'you pulled your phone out and said let\'s educate ourselves today. Looked up verbal abuse and told them it\'s not illegal.'
signals.cp2_confirmation: Q1 confirmed: Children ages 10, 6, 1 home in small house. Eli confirms children asked about verbal abuse and he looked it up and explained. Out-of-character incident — sinus infection, steroids, sleep deprivation. Eli apologized. Eli-direction Sev-2 (mitigated but confirmed).
signals.arc_state_after: ACTIVE (5 signals) — final signal; arc unresolved at dataset close
signals.note: Only bilateral signal in the pass. Eli-direction: children directly present during adult argument + children involved in adult legal-definitional discussion (verbal abuse). Melissa-direction: trauma narrative deployed as ongoing visit-refusal engine through Apr 2026.

compound_patterns.description: Counselor relay weaponization — therapeutic content used as inadequacy ammunition + Olivia as parental support judge
Fear relay parentification — court order inverted as endangerment framing; child as safety determinator
Police report child-exposure — legal enforcement context disclosed to child; documented behavioral impact
Explicit parentification — 'her choice not to go'; court-compliance decision transferred to child
Bilateral hiding incident — Eli-direction: child exposure to adult argument + verbal abuse framing; Melissa-direction: amplification deployment (COMP-RF04-029, RF-09 S011)

arc_synthesis.primary_mechanism_melissa: Parentification of visit-compliance decisions: Melissa consistently frames Olivia's court-ordered visit refusals as Olivia's autonomous choice, relieving Melissa of enforcement responsibility while burdening Olivia with adult legal decisions. Text-documented in ROW-14841 ('it was her choice'). Confirmed escalating from fear-relay framing (B-028) through explicit autonomy-framing (B-029) through police-report disclosure (B-029).
arc_synthesis.primary_mechanism_eli: Feb 2026 domestic argument: children directly present and affected; Eli's responsive explanation of 'verbal abuse' to frightened children constitutes adult legal framing in children's presence. Single out-of-character incident; mitigated by context, health factors, apology. Sev-2 bilateral.
arc_synthesis.stabilizer_pattern: CP2 Q3 confirmed: Olivia serves as Melissa's emotional stabilizer. ADD-006 context: each documented parentification signal reflects a sustained behavioral pattern, not isolated incidents. The therapeutic relationship deployment (S001 B-027) is the most direct text-record evidence of the stabilizer mechanism.
arc_synthesis.police_report_impact: S003 (B-029): Police report disclosure to child is the highest-consequence RF-11 signal from a child-welfare perspective. Telling a 10-year-old that her father 'called the police' about her visit refusal places the child in the adult legal conflict and produces documented behavioral impact (week-long silence toward Eli). This signal is likely to be the most significant RF-11 item for GAL analysis.
arc_synthesis.phase_pattern: Zero Phase A signals — cooperative baseline period, no RF-11 behavioral indicators visible in text record. One Phase B signal (B-027 counselor relay, Aug 2025) preceding Phase C escalation. Four Phase C signals concentrated in Oct-Feb 2025-2026 window — all within the visit-refusal cascade period. Pattern intensifies with legal/custody pressure.
arc_synthesis.therapist_pattern: ADD-004 behavioral note: Melissa engages therapists then terminates when called out (Q6). This pattern itself is clinically significant — suggests strategic therapeutic engagement rather than genuine treatment for Olivia. Cannot be directly flagged from text record but is critical context for GAL evaluation of Olivia's therapeutic history.
arc_synthesis.cross_rf_density: 5 of 5 signals are compound patterns. Most structurally embedded in RF-02 (visit-refusal cascade) — RF-11 cannot be analyzed in isolation from the gatekeeping arc. RF-04 involved in 4 of 5 compounds. RF-09 compound in S005 (Feb 2026 amplification triple). RF-08 compound in S001 (counselor relay).
